title: poterium sanguisorba subsp. glaucescens
intro: Poterium sanguisorba subsp. glaucescens, commonly known as the red or bloodwort, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It is a perennial herb native to Europe and western Asia, valued for its medicinal properties and various health benefits. The plant contains flavonoids, phenolic acids, and tannins, which have been traditionally used to treat gastrointestinal issues, reduce inflammation, and support cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, Poterium sanguisorba subsp. glaucescens can be found in teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to aid in digestion, reduce bleeding, and promote overall well-being.
